title: Silent, Silent Night
content:
Silent, silent night,
    Quench the holy light
    Of thy torches bright;

    For possessed of Day
    Thousand spirits stray
    That sweet joys betray.

    Why should joys be sweet
    Used with deceit,
    Nor with sorrows meet?

    But an honest joy
    Does itself destroy
    For a harlot coy.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Blake, titled "Silent, Silent Night", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
